title: Freeze on Death with Loss Reason Display
content:
## Overview When any snake dies, the game enters a **death freeze** instead of immediately jumping to the Game Over screen. The board stays visible and frozen, the fatal collision cell is highlighted in amber on the board, a pulsing banner names the loss reason, and the game waits for any key press before advancing to the normal Game Over screen. This applies to all modes: human single-player, bot-driven single-player, and bot-vs-bot. Before this feature, `endGame()` / `endGameTwoPlayer()` were called immediately on death, which called `showScreen('game-over')` and set `display:none` on `#game`, hiding the canvas entirely. The fatal head cell was never drawn because collision was checked before `this.snake.unshift(head)` and `draw()` was never called on the death path. --- ## Architecture ### New instance fields (`src/main.ts`) Four fields added alongside the existing `deathCause` field (~line 154): | Field | Type | Purpose | |---|---|---| | `deathReason` | `'wall' \| 'self' \| 'opponent' \| 'head-to-head' \| 'starved'` | Drives the banner text | | `deathCells` | `Array<{ x: number; y: number; who: 1 \| 2 }>` | Fatal head cell(s) to highlight; empty for starvation | | `awaitingDeathAck` | `boolean` | True during the freeze | | `pendingFinalize` | `(() => void) \| null` | Deferred end-game callback | The existing `deathCause: 'collision' | 'starved'` field is **unchanged** — it still drives the Game Over title and winner nuance in `endGame`/`endGameTwoPlayer`. All four new fields are reset in `startNewGame` next to `this.deathCause = 'collision'` (~line 1104), and `hideDeathBanner()` is called beside the pause-hide (~line 1110) so a restart never leaves a stuck banner or stale alarm cell. ### `beginDeathFreeze(finalize: () => void)` (near end-game functions, before line 1801) 1. Sets `isGameOver = true`, `awaitingDeathAck = true`, `pendingFinalize = finalize`. 2. Calls `stopAutoRotate()` and clears the game loop (`clearInterval` + `this.gameLoop = null`) — same lines `endGame` runs, idempotent when `endGame` re-runs them at finalize time. 3. Hides `#starvation-warning` so it doesn't overlap the banner. 4. Calls `this.draw()` to render the frozen board with death cell highlight(s). 5. Calls `this.showDeathBanner()`. ### Four death sites rewired (`src/main.ts`) | Site | Location | `deathReason` | `deathCells` | Callback | |---|---|---|---|---| | Single-player collision | ~line 1161 | `inBounds(head) ? 'self' : 'wall'` | `[{x:head.x, y:head.y, who:1}]` | `() => this.endGame()` | | Single-player starvation | ~line 1183 | `'starved'` | `[]` | `() => this.endGame()` | | Two-player collision | ~line 1231 | derived from collision booleans | `head1` if p1Dead, `head2` if p2Dead | `() => this.endGameTwoPlayer(p1Dead, p2Dead)` | | Two-player starvation | ~line 1275 | `'starved'` | `[]` | `() => this.endGameTwoPlayer(p1Starved, p2Starved)` | For two-player collision, `deathReason` is derived from the already-computed booleans: `headToHead ? 'head-to-head' : (p1HitsWall||p2HitsWall) ? 'wall' : (p1HitsSelf||p2HitsSelf) ? 'self' : 'opponent'`. Per-snake reasons `r1`/`r2` are also computed for the banner text. ### Death cell rendering in `draw()` (after the object-draw loop, ~line 628) Only active when `awaitingDeathAck`. For each cell in `deathCells`, an amber/alarm highlight block is drawn **after** the main painter-sorted loop so it paints on top of any coincident body or opponent segment (necessary for self/opponent/head-to-head deaths that overlap an existing segment). **Bounds handling** branches on the grid rectangle (`x < 0 || x >= gridSize || y < 0 || y >= gridSize`): - **Off the grid rectangle (wall death):** the true cell projects mostly off-canvas (canvas is sized to grid + 20px padding). Fallback: draw the alarm block at the snake's current head (`snake[0]`/`snake2[0]`, the last valid cell), **and** also at the true OOB coordinate so a sliver pokes past the border edge as an "off the edge" cue. - **Within the grid rectangle (self / opponent / head-to-head, or Iron Snake interior void):** projects on-canvas; draw directly. This correctly shows Iron Snake deaths where the head entered the interior void. Starvation (`deathCells = []`) is a no-op here — the freeze shows the last live frame plus the banner only. Death cells are re-read on every `draw()` call, so the highlight persists across window resize and any redraws that occur while frozen. ### Banner DOM and CSS - **`index.html`:** `
` added inside `.canvas-wrap` as a sibling after `#starvation-warning` (~line 97). Overlays the canvas without shifting layout; no `showScreen` needed. - **`src/style.css`:** `.death-banner` modeled on `.starvation-warning` (lines 184–206) but centered (`top:50%; transform:translate(-50%,-50%)`), larger, alarm-red, `pointer-events:none`, with its own pulse keyframe. **Banner copy:** | `deathReason` | Text | |---|---| | `wall` | "Hit the wall!" | | `self` | "Ran into itself!" | | `opponent` | "Crashed into the other snake!" | | `head-to-head` | "Head-on collision!" | | `starved` | "Starved!" | Two-player mode composes with P1/P2 labels using per-snake `r1`/`r2` reasons. All messages append " — Press any key". Two methods near `updateStarvationWarning` (~line 1750): `showDeathBanner()` builds and displays the text; `hideDeathBanner()` re-hides it. ### Acknowledgement branch in `handleKeyPress` (`src/main.ts`) Inserted at the very top of `handleKeyPress`, after the INPUT/TEXTAREA/SELECT guard (~line 752) and **before** the `Enter` branch (~line 754): ```typescript if (this.awaitingDeathAck) { if (e.repeat || e.key === 'Shift' || e.key === 'Control' || e.key === 'Alt' || e.key === 'Meta') return e.preventDefault() this.awaitingDeathAck = false this.hideDeathBanner() const finalize = this.pendingFinalize this.pendingFinalize = null finalize?.() return } ``` Ordering is critical: this fires before `Enter`/`r`/`Escape` branches, so during the freeze the first key dismisses to Game Over rather than triggering restart or menu. After `finalize()` runs, `showScreen('game-over')` is called and normal Game Over key handling resumes unchanged. `e.repeat` and bare modifiers are ignored to prevent a stray held key from skipping the freeze instantly. **Any key dismisses the freeze** — including camera/view keys. This is the simplest behavior and matches "until a key is pressed." > **Note:** The commit description mentions that camera keys (rotate, overhead, perspective) were whitelisted to fall through during the freeze as a "death cam" feature. The later plan document specifies that any key dismisses. The plan (newer) governs: any key dismisses. --- ## Files Modified | File | Changes | |---|---| | `src/main.ts` | New fields; `beginDeathFreeze`, `showDeathBanner`, `hideDeathBanner`; four death sites rewired; death-cell rendering in `draw()`; ack branch in `handleKeyPress`; resets in `startNewGame` | | `index.html` | `#death-banner` div inside `.canvas-wrap` | | `src/style.css` | `.death-banner` styles and pulse keyframe | --- ## Behavioral Notes - `endGame`/`endGameTwoPlayer` run at ack-time (key press), not at the death instant. `saveHighScore`, `maybePromptForEntry`, and bvb `addEntry` calls therefore fire when the key is pressed. Nothing depends on them firing earlier. Closing the tab during the freeze skips score recording — acceptable. - The freeze is purely additive: all winner resolution, high-score saving, and leaderboard logic inside `endGame`/`endGameTwoPlayer` runs unchanged. - Iron Snake interior void deaths, overhead view, and both-snakes-dying-on-the-same-tick (draw / head-to-head) are all handled by the render/bounds logic.
